title: esus’ first recorded prayer part 4
scripture_text: “At that time Jesus said, “I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, for this is what you were pleased to do. All things have been committed to me by my Father. No one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal him. “Come to me all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. for my yoke is easy and my burden is light.”
devotional:
2. Not only was Jesus gentle, he was also humble in heart. Jesus was able to wear his yoke because he had no pride in his heart. The word used for humble was used to describe a person of no standing in the community. Jesus did not think of himself in a proud way; deep within his heart he hand a humble attitude. Pride encourages the heavy load; it puts pressure and expectations on people.

Jesus is saying "Do not fear my yoke for I am not like your previous masters. I am gentle and humble. You do not have to carry the heavy load the worlds expectations has placed upon you."

Look how Jesus describes his yoke, "For my yoke is easy and my burden is light. "The word for easy is Chrestos. This word means to be kind. When we allow Jesus to place his yoke upon us, then we are responding appropriately to his kindness. It is kind of Jesus to want to be our Lord and have complete control over our lives. For he knows that we will only discover life to the full if we bow to his rule in our lives, in the same way He bowed to his Fathers will. The word Chrestos was also used to describe mature wine. (Luke 5:39.) Wine that has no bitterness in it. When we discover the yoke of Jesus, it is not a bitter experience; the effects of Christ's yoke will remove all bitterness from our hearts, why? Because we submit to Christ believing that he is sovereign.

Jesus concludes with saying "My burden is light." Bowing to the will of God does not mean that our lives become carefree or careless. Bowing to the yoke of Christ means that we will be equipped to carry the right kind of burdens. The burdens that Jesus was speaking of here could be hooked onto the yoke. Many people go through life "weary and burdened" and are unable to carry the God designed burdens we need to carry if we are going to have a fulfilled life. Only people, who have experienced the rest of Jesus, can bow to his yoke and carry his burden.

Jesus gives this invitation so that those who come to him will find rest. The word for rest is. " Anapausin " denotes a temporary rest, a respite, e.g. 'of soldiers who rested to prepare to go back into battle,' to refresh us for the work that lies ahead. This rest is the result of intimacy when we respond to Jesus' invitation "come unto me."

Jesus found rest when he came to his Father. If we do not learn to find rest in God, and be refreshed by being with him, we will be unable to bow to his yoke and carry his burden, and we will end up weary and tired under our own burdens.
prayer: Father help me find real rest.
html:
esus’ first recorded prayer part 4

Matthew 11:25-30 J
“At that time Jesus said, “I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, for this is what you were pleased to do. All things have been committed to me by my Father. No one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal him. “Come to me all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. for my yoke is easy and my burden is light.”

2. Not only was Jesus gentle, he was also humble in heart. Jesus was able to wear his yoke because he had no pride in his heart. The word used for humble was used to describe a person of no standing in the community. Jesus did not think of himself in a proud way; deep within his heart he hand a humble attitude. Pride encourages the heavy load; it puts pressure and expectations on people.

Jesus is saying "Do not fear my yoke for I am not like your previous masters. I am gentle and humble. You do not have to carry the heavy load the worlds expectations has placed upon you."

Look how Jesus describes his yoke, "For my yoke is easy and my burden is light. "The word for easy is Chrestos. This word means to be kind. When we allow Jesus to place his yoke upon us, then we are responding appropriately to his kindness. It is kind of Jesus to want to be our Lord and have complete control over our lives. For he knows that we will only discover life to the full if we bow to his rule in our lives, in the same way He bowed to his Fathers will. The word Chrestos was also used to describe mature wine. (Luke 5:39.) Wine that has no bitterness in it. When we discover the yoke of Jesus, it is not a bitter experience; the effects of Christ's yoke will remove all bitterness from our hearts, why? Because we submit to Christ believing that he is sovereign.

Jesus concludes with saying "My burden is light." Bowing to the will of God does not mean that our lives become carefree or careless. Bowing to the yoke of Christ means that we will be equipped to carry the right kind of burdens. The burdens that Jesus was speaking of here could be hooked onto the yoke. Many people go through life "weary and burdened" and are unable to carry the God designed burdens we need to carry if we are going to have a fulfilled life. Only people, who have experienced the rest of Jesus, can bow to his yoke and carry his burden.

Jesus gives this invitation so that those who come to him will find rest. The word for rest is. " Anapausin " denotes a temporary rest, a respite, e.g. 'of soldiers who rested to prepare to go back into battle,' to refresh us for the work that lies ahead. This rest is the result of intimacy when we respond to Jesus' invitation "come unto me."

Jesus found rest when he came to his Father. If we do not learn to find rest in God, and be refreshed by being with him, we will be unable to bow to his yoke and carry his burden, and we will end up weary and tired under our own burdens.

Prayer: Father help me find real rest.
